data.title: AIG appoints Christopher Flatt as chief underwriting officer
data.description:
• American International Group (NYSE:AIG) announced on Thursday the appointment of Christopher Flatt as chief underwriting officer, effective immediately.
  • Flatt currently serves as the head of AIG North America alternative channels.
  • Flatt, who will report directly to CEO Peter Zaffino, succeeds Christopher Schaper.
  • Schaper was appointed the chief risk officer of the multi-line insurer in November 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Retail juggernaut: Costco keeps winning market share as Gen Y and Gen Z join the club
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (NASDAQ:COST) has energized bulls with a strong December sales report. The highlight of the report may have been that the retailer's core U.S. comparable sales improved 9.8% year-over-year and were up 23.6% on a 3-year stack. The Costco (NASDAQ:COST) numbers were impressive even after factoring in a calendar shift that saw Thanksgiving, Black Friday, and Cyber Monday fall later on the calendar.

Sales of non-food items jumped by a high-teens percentage in December, led by strength in jewelry, gift cards, toys, and seasonal items. Fresh food sales rose by a high single-digit percentage during the month, led by meat and produce. The pharmacy, optical, and food court categories all saw stronger year-over-year growth than in November.

Jefferies analyst Corey Tarlowe said the prospects still remain for Costco (COST) to deliver comparable sales one to two points above historical levels based on a channel shift from traditional grocery, department stores, and specialty retail, as well as higher growth among Gen Y/Z demos with a stronger skew toward club offerings. Tarlowe said Costco (COST) is also benefiting from bigger baskets as customers increasingly shop in categories beyond food.

UBS also reiterated its bull rating on Costco (COST) after the "meaningful acceleration" in December sales. "Overall, we think the company continues to deliver compelling value, quality, and newness," updated the analyst team.

Evercore ISI analyst Greg Melich noted that with food and consumable inflation proving to be resilient, the firm believes that Costco's (COST) loyalty-driven extreme value model is in goodposition to deliver steady share gain.

Costco (COST) gained 1.9% in postmarket trading on Wednesday, and could be positioned to make another run at the $1,000 closing level. The high mark for the stock was on December 11, 2024, when it closed at $994.69.

Retail watch: The consensus is that Amazon (AMZN), Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), and Costco (COST) will collectively account for an even higher percentage of retail sales in 2025 based on recent sales reports from the Big Four of Retail.

data.title: Plains All American Pipeline raises dividend by 19.7% to $0.38
data.description:
• Plains All American Pipeline (NASDAQ:PAA) declares $0.38/share quarterly dividend, 19.7% increase from prior dividend of $0.32.
  • Forward yield 8.12%
  • Payable Feb. 14; for shareholders of record Jan. 31; ex-div Jan. 31.
  • See PAA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Rigel receives orphan drug designation by the FDA for its myelodysplastic syndrome treatment
data.description:
• Rigel Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:RIGL) announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has granted Orphan Drug designation to R289 for the treatment of myelodysplastic syndromes.
  • R289 was previously granted Fast Track designation by the FDA for the treatment of patients with previously-treated transfusion dependent lower-risk MDS.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amwell to sell Amwell Psychiatric Care unit to Avel eCare
data.description:
• Amwell (NYSE:AMWL) said it has sold its Amwell Psychiatric Care business to Avel eCare.
  • Avel eCare will be buying the APC business for ~$21M in cash.
  • The sale includes the APC technology, personnel, and Asana.
  • “The divestment of APC will enable us to increase focus on our unified, world class digital care platform. The transaction strengthens our balance sheet while emphasizing our focus on growing accretive software contribution in our product mix,” said CEO, Ido Schoenberg.
  • Amwell also reaffirmed its guidance for 2024.

data.title: Independence Realty Trust expands unsecured credit facility
data.description:
• Independence Realty Trust (NYSE:IRT) said its operating partnership, Independence Realty Operating amended and restated an unsecured credit facility.
  • The new facility upped the borrowing capacity under IRT’s existing revolver from $500 million to $750 million and also extended the maturity date from January 2026 to January 2029.
  • The proceeds from the expanded revolver will be used for general corporate purposes.
  • The amended and restated unsecured credit facility also cut the margin on IRT’s existing $200 million term loan, and the borrowings under the $200 million term loan now bear interest at SOFR plus 0.80% to 1.60%.

data.title: Osisko Development announces senior management update
data.description:
• Osisko Development (NYSE:ODV) has announced the appointment of David Rouleau as Vice President, Project Development.
  • This appointment aligns with the Co.'s strategic focus on building an operations-driven team to advance its flagship 100%-owned Cariboo Gold Project, located in central British Columbia, Canada.
  • The Co. also announces that Éric Tremblay, COO, is leaving the Co. effective immediately to pursue other professional commitments in the mining sector.
  • In addition to his appointed role, Mr. Rouleau will provide senior leadership support during the transition period until a permanent COO is appointed, and he will take an active role in advancing operational readiness efforts for the Cariboo Gold Project.

data.title: Uniti commences offering of $589M secured fiber network revenue term notes
data.description:
• Specialized REIT Uniti Group (NASDAQ:UNIT) announced on Thursday the commencement of an offering of $589M worth of secured fiber network revenue term notes.
  • The offering will be conducted by the Uniti subsidiaries, Uniti Fiber ABS Issuer and Uniti Fiber TRS Issuer.
  • The notes are expected to have a repayment date in April 2030.
  • The notes will be secured by certain fiber network assets and related customer contracts in the State of Florida and the Gulf Coast region of Louisiana, Mississippi and Alabama.
  • The net proceeds are intended to be used to repay and terminate the company's existing ABS bridge facility and for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lumentum gets top pick tag in networking sector at Needham
data.description:
Needham named Lumentum (NASDAQ:LITE) as top pick for 2025 across its Networking and Cloud Communication sectors on surging AI investment.

The firm also added Lumentum to the Needham conviction list, replacing Harmonic (HLIT), while keeping a Buy rating and raising LITE's price target to $110.

Analysts led by Ryan Koontz said they expect Lumentum to benefit from continued strong growth in its Datacom business driven by surging AI infrastructure investments, and a sustained recovery in its telecom business, also largely driven by data center Wide Area Network, or WAN connectivity.

The analysts see expanding externally modulated lasers, or EML, production capacity as a major catalyst with the first mid-2025 tranche mostly priced in, but a second capacity tranche providing strong upside to 2026 estimates.

In addition, the analysts said that a likely higher U.S. tariffs on China open an opportunity to gain meaningful share in Datacom transceivers, or XCVRs.

data.title: CyberArk named top cybersecurity pick for 2025 by Needham
data.description:
CyberArk Software (NASDAQ:CYBR) was named the top stock for 2025 in the cybersecurity space by Needham Securities, amid continuing momentum.

"CYBR outperformed in CY24 (+54% vs. 27% IGV) but shares are well positioned for sustained momentum in CY25 driven by an accelerating threat environment, robust new logo growth, upside from Venafi cross-sell and synergies, a favorable consensus model setup, and continued strong execution against an advantageous competitive backdrop," Needham analyst Matt Dezort wrote in a note to clients.

Dezort also named CyberArk to Needham's Conviction List and upped his price target on the stock to $410 from $360.

Looking to 2025, Dezort said he thinks the initial annual recurring revenue guidance is likely to top the $1.39B estimate, as it includes the Venafi acquisition, but also implies a "modest" year-over-year decline in the company's core offerings, which is unlikely.

"CYBR has offered initial ARR guidance 1%-2%+ ahead of consensus each of the last few years, ultimately finishing the year 5%+ above original guidance," Deozrt added. "While we see more modest organic upside to estimates in CY25, the setup for beat and raise quarters throughout remains favorable, and we see at least mid-20% ARR growth as achievable."
